HTTP stands for **HyperText Transfer Protocol**. It is the foundation of data communication on the World Wide Web, enabling clients (such as web browsers) to request and receive hypermedia content (like HTML documents, images, videos, etc.) from servers. 

When you type a URL into a browser, an HTTP request is sent to the server, which then responds with the requested data. A secure version of HTTP, called **HTTPS**, uses encryption (via SSL/TLS) to protect data in transit. 

The term "HyperText" refers to text that contains links ("hypertext links") to other texts, a concept central to navigating the web.
